comment: Production exec approvals for OpenClaw VPS deployment
comment_security: LLM agents can ONLY run pre-approved commands. This prevents: shell injection, data exfiltration, container escape, privilege escalation.
comment_usage: Mount at: /data/.openclaw/exec-approvals.json inside container. Or use 'openclaw config set' CLI to modify.

defaults.comment_security_mode: allowlist = only approved commands run; ask = prompt user; askFallback = deny if no UI (LINE has no UI, so defaults to deny)

main.comment_note: LINE channel has no approval UI, so ask='on-miss' + askFallback='deny' means: unapproved commands are silently denied

allowlist.comment: Safe system info commands (read-only, no side effects)
Process listing (read-only, shows running processes only this container)
Cron job inspection (read-only, lists scheduled tasks)
Tree directory listing (read-only, shows directory structure)
Data processing (read-only stdin, safe for pipes)
Docker read-only inspection (allowed: ps, logs, stats, inspect. NOT: exec, run)

comment_blocklist: Commands that are NEVER allowed, even if in allowlist. These are security-critical.

never_allow.reason: Allows breaking out of container to run commands in sibling containers or host
User switching with potential privilege escalation
